(a) make the Modified Version available to the Copyright Holder of the Standard Version, under the Original License
(b) ensure that installation of your Modified Version does not prevent the user installing or running the Standard Version
(c) allow anyone who receives a copy of the Modified Version to make the Source form ... available to others
(5) You may Distribute Compiled forms of the Standard Version without the Source, provided that you include complete instructions on how to get the Source of the Standard Version
(6) You may Distribute a Modified Version in Compiled form without the Source, provided that you comply with Section 4 with respect to the Source of the Modified Version

external_references.summary: FSF classes Artistic 2.0 as a free software license with conditional source availability rather than strict copyleft; source disclosure is one of several alternative compliance paths under sections 4-5.

conditions.commentary: Source disclosure is conditional. Section 4 gives three alternatives for source distributions (contribute upstream, install-side-by-side with renaming, or grant downstream source redistribution rights). Section 5 requires instructions to obtain the source when distributing compiled-only Standard Versions. So source availability is required in some distribution paths but optional overall, which is why this is grey rather than required.

citations.note: (4)(a) make the Modified Version available to the Copyright Holder ... under the Original License
(4)(c) allow anyone who receives a copy of the Modified Version to make the Source form ... available to others under
(i) the Original License or (ii) a license that permits the licensee to freely copy, modify and redistribute ... using the same licensing terms
(6) You may Distribute a Modified Version in Compiled form without the Source, provided that you comply with Section 4 with respect to the Source of the Modified Version

external_references.summary: FSF describes Artistic 2.0 as a free software license whose GPL compatibility depends on the chosen compliance path. The license allows relicensing Modified Versions under compatible terms or keeping the Original License.

conditions.commentary: Split-license flexibility. Under section 4 the distributor may choose to keep the Original License, contribute the modifications back so they live under the Original License, rename the Modified Version, or relicense under any license that preserves the same downstream copy/modify/redistribute rights. Because relicensing to a compatible alternative is explicitly permitted, this is grey rather than a strict same-license share-alike.
